global_context.scene_description: A cinematic editorial photograph (or photorealistic editorial illustration with photographic depth) of a single ASH-branded HVAC service truck parked at the curb of a regular Phoenix-style suburban home, late afternoon golden-hour light. The truck is the hero element, parked at a slight 3/4 angle so the side branding panel is visible. NOT a logo design — the truck looks like a real working service truck with subtle wraps and visible 'ASH COOLING & HEATING' lettering on the side panel and ladder racks visible on top. ONE technician in a clean uniform is at the back of the truck, having just closed the rear doors, holding a small clipboard, walking toward the driver's side. In the background mid-distance: the Phoenix-style home, single-story stucco with desert landscaping, a brand-new exterior AC compressor unit visible at the side of the house with morning dew or just-installed cleanliness. Late afternoon golden-hour sun raking from the left, long shadows, warm orange-gold tones, palm tree silhouettes in the deep background, distant mountain hint. Mood: a clean workday completed, professional, calm, simple. Single overlay text element top-third: bold white sans-serif 'STARTED AT 8. DONE BY 5.' as an editorial banner. Small subhead in white italic: 'New AC. It's just a workday.' Editorial cinematic photography lighting, NOT documentary snapshot, NOT cartoon, NOT flat infographic. Composition feels like an Apple commercial frame or a high-end trade campaign — clean, intentional, golden, the truck and tech the only humans/vehicles in frame.
